decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly build a board presence with efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria, Skyclave Apparition, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck also utilizes ramp spells like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to accelerate mana production. Collected Company serves as a powerful card advantage engine, allowing the player to dig for more threats. Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides a powerful finisher to close out games. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and efficient creatures while disrupting their game plan with cards like Enduring Innocence.

The deck focuses on controlling the game using a wide variety of spells and abilities to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up the battlefield to your advantage. The inclusion of Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a central card allows for card advantage and selection, while the companion Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional utility. The deck has a strong mana base to support its diverse array of spells and powerful creatures, ensuring that you can adapt to any situation and outlast your opponent in a battle of attrition.

This deck focuses on a fast and aggressive strategy, aiming to quickly deploy Arclight Phoenix and other spells to deal damage to the opponent. The deck is built around casting multiple spells in one turn to bring Arclight Phoenix back from the graveyard and pressure the opponent. The deck includes a mix of efficient removal spells, card draw spells, and cantrips to dig for important cards. The mana base is optimized to support the aggressive strategy and allow for consistent casting of spells.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. By utilizing cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to maintain board presence while controlling the opponent's threats. The mana base is designed to support the early game aggression of the deck, while also providing flexibility for casting powerful spells like Elspeth, Storm Slayer. Overall, this deck focuses on curving out efficiently and applying pressure to secure victories in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using cheap and efficient spells like Burst Lightning and Fiery Temper, as well as creatures like Marauding Mako and Seasoned Hallowblade. The deck also includes cards that provide additional value or protection, such as Fear of Missing Out and Sheltered by Ghosts. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive strategy, with a mix of red and white sources to cast the aggressive creatures and burn spells efficiently.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Greasefang Parhelion combo to its maximum potential, using cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Monument to Endurance to protect and enhance the combo. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board while Erode deals with any problematic permanents. The mana base ensures a smooth curve for the combo pieces to come together efficiently. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

The main strategy of this deck is to use the synergy between Greasefang Parhelion and other cards such as Parhelion II, Fable of the Mirror-Breaker, and Bloodtithe Harvester to quickly generate value and overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board, as well as disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Torch the Tower to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a focus on combo plays and aggressive card advantage, this deck aims to outpace opponents and win through powerful synergies.

The main concept of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck is to control the game through disruption and removal spells while utilizing powerful creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to dominate the board. The deck aims to disrupt opponents' strategies with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while also using cards like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to remove threats. The mana base is set up to support the deck's strategies and ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, the deck focuses on controlling the game and out-valuing opponents.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly play low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to deal damage to the opponent as quickly as possible. Using burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, along with evasion from Flying with cards like Stormchaser's Talent, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and overwhelming them with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. The deck also includes card draw and selection with Sleight of Hand and Academic Dispute to help maintain a consistent flow of threats.

The key strategy of this deck is to maintain control of the game by using a combination of removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. The Rock archetype focuses on grinding out value through card advantage and efficient removal, while also deploying threats like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to pressure the opponent. The deck also includes utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to enhance its overall performance.

This deck focuses on using a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and powerful creatures to control the game and grind out a victory. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Abrupt Decay to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse provide a strong board presence. The deck also includes cards like Culling Ritual and Tear Asunder to deal with various types of threats. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can secure a win.

The key concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, along with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck also aims to generate value with cards like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Professor Dellian Fel, while using powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game. Additionally, the deck utilizes lands like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Blooming Marsh to ensure smooth mana development and consistency in casting its spells.

The main focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to aggressively play creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria slow down the opponent's spells, while Skyclave Apparition removes threats from the board. Collected Company helps to flood the board with creatures quickly, while Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides additional removal and a powerful win condition. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with an efficient and aggressive creature-based strategy, backed up by disruptive spells and removal.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources through hand disruption and removal spells while building up its own board presence with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. It also utilizes lands like Mutavault and Llanowar Wastes to ramp up mana quickly and cast powerful spells like Culling Ritual. With a combination of creature removal, hand disruption, and powerful threats, this deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through attrition.

This deck aims to control the game through disruption and removal spells while setting up a board presence with resilient and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City. It utilizes a mix of hand disruption, removal, and versatile creatures to outvalue and outlast opponents in a long game. The deck packs a solid mana base to support its spells and creatures, with a focus on generating value and card advantage to grind out victories over time.

The main idea of this UW Control deck is to maintain control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card draw. The deck aims to stall the opponent's strategy, disrupt their plays, and generate card advantage through various means such as Consult the Star Charts, Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, and Narset, Parter of Veils. By controlling the board and hand, the deck can outvalue the opponent and eventually win through powerful threats like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Ultima.
